template_name: Standard Prescription Format - Newborn Metabolic Screening

condition.name: Newborn Metabolic Screening
purpose: Detect rare but serious metabolic and genetic disorders before symptoms appear.
Enable timely intervention to prevent severe disability, organ damage, or death.
overview: Many inborn errors of metabolism have silent onset initially.
A simple heel-prick dried blood spot test can screen for multiple disorders efficiently.

sample_collection_timing.ideal_time: After 24 hours of birth and before 72 hours
sample_collection_timing.if_early_discharge: If sample taken before 24 hours, repeat sample at 48-72 hours
sample_collection_timing.note: These special groups may require repeat screening

screening_method.sample_type: Dried Blood Spot (DBS) on filter paper card

early_treatment_benefits.benefit: Prevents anemia, infections, and stroke complications

result_interpretation.action: Start disease-specific treatment as soon as possible

red_flags: Family history of unexplained neonatal deaths
parent_counseling: Explain that newborn metabolic screening is preventive, not diagnostic. — नवजात की मेटाबोलिक जांच बीमारी पहचानने के लिए नहीं, बचाव के लिए है।
Discuss that false-positive results can occur and are clarified by confirmatory testing. — कभी-कभी गलत रिपोर्ट आ सकती है, जो दोबारा जांच से साफ़ हो जाती है।
Emphasize that early detection significantly improves long-term outcomes. — जल्दी पता लगने से बच्चे का भविष्य बहुत बेहतर होता है।
If screened too early, repeat testing may be needed. — अगर जन्म के तुरंत बाद जांच हुई तो दोबारा करानी पड़ सकती है।
Heel prick test is safe and causes only brief discomfort. — एड़ी से खून लेना सुरक्षित है और थोड़ी ही देर तकलीफ़ होती है।
quick_checklist_for_opd: Confirm whether newborn metabolic screening has been done.
Arrange retesting if early discharge or sample collected before 24 hours.
Track borderline or abnormal results closely.
flow_logic: If diagnosis confirmed -> early treatment initiation

standard_prescription_format.title: Standard Prescription / Advice Sheet - Newborn Metabolic Screening
provisional_assessment_options: Abnormal screening report - confirmatory evaluation advised

investigation_or_order_template.timing: After 24 hours and before 72 hours of life
At 48-72 hours if first sample taken before 24 hours

if_not_done: Arrange newborn metabolic screening immediately as per age eligibility.
if_sample_early: Repeat screening sample at appropriate age.
if_borderline_or_positive: Specialist referral as per suspected disorder.

parent_advice: Heel-prick screening is important even if baby appears healthy.
Keep report safely and bring it on follow-up.
Do not delay confirmatory testing if report is abnormal.
Seek urgent care if baby becomes sick.

warning_signs: Poor feeding or vomiting — दूध न पीना या उल्टी
Excessive sleepiness or lethargy — बहुत ज़्यादा सोना या सुस्ती
Seizures or abnormal movements — दौरे या असामान्य हरकत
Unusual body odor — शरीर से अजीब गंध
Failure to gain weight — वज़न न बढ़ना
Jaundice that is worsening — पीलिया बढ़ना
expected_course: Normal screening results require no further action. Borderline results need repeat testing. Abnormal or positive results require urgent confirmatory diagnostic testing and disease-specific treatment. With early detection and treatment, many metabolic disorders have excellent outcomes.
key_clinical_points: Ideal sample timing is 24-72 hours after birth.
Samples collected before 24 hours may give false negatives and must be repeated.
Preterm babies, sick neonates, and transfused babies require repeat screening.
Congenital hypothyroidism is the most common condition detected — early levothyroxine prevents intellectual disability.
G6PD deficiency screening helps prevent hemolytic crises with trigger avoidance.
Parents must be counseled to keep results safely and follow up promptly.
